double last_logged_lux = 0.0;

// Function prototypes
int init_i2c();
double read_lux();
int set_led_brightness(int brightness);
int log_lux(double lux);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    if (signal == SIGINT) {
        printf("Caught SIGINT, exiting gracefully.\n");
        set_led_brightness(0); // Turn off LED
        i2cClose(i2c_handle);
        gpioTerminate();
        exit(0);
    }
}

// Initialize I2C communication
int init_i2c() {
    i2c_handle = i2cOpen(1, I2C_ADDR, 0); // Bus 1, address 0x23, no flags
    if (i2c_handle < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening I2C device: %d\n", i2c_handle);
        return -1;
    }
    return 0;
}

// Read lux value from BH1750 sensor
double read_lux() {
    int raw_value;
    double lux;
    unsigned char config_cmd[1] = {0x10}; // High resolution mode

    if (i2cWriteByte(i2c_handle, config_cmd[0]) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error writing to I2C device\n");
        return -1.0;
    }
    gpioDelay(200); // Wait for measurement (at least 180ms in high resolution mode)

    raw_value = i2cReadWordData(i2c_handle, 0x00); // Read MSB and LSB
    if (raw_value < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading from I2C device\n");
        return -1.0;
    }

    lux = (double)raw_value / 1.2; // Conversion formula from datasheet
    return lux;
}

// Set LED brightness using PWM
int set_led_brightness(int brightness) {
    if (brightness < 0 || brightness > PWM_RANGE) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Brightness value out of range (0-255)\n");
        return -1;
    }
    gpioPWM(GPIO_LED, brightness);
    return 0;
}

// Log lux value to file
int log_lux(double lux) {
    FILE *fp;
    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm tm = *localtime(&t);
    char timestamp[20];

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", &tm);

    fp = fopen("light_log.txt", "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file\n");
        return -1;
    }

    fprintf(fp, "%s Lux: %.2f\n", timestamp, lux);
    fclose(fp);
    return 0;
}

int main() {
    // Initialize pigpio
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Initialize GPIO for LED
    gpioSetMode(GPIO_LED, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Initialize I2C
    if (init_i2c() != 0) {
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Set PWM range
    gpioSetPWMrange(GPIO_LED, PWM_RANGE);
    gpioSetPWMfrequency(GPIO_LED, 1000); // 1 kHz

    // Set up signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    double current_lux;
    int brightness;

    while (1) {
        current_lux = read_lux();
        if (current_lux < 0) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error reading lux value, retrying I2C initialization.\n");
            i2cClose(i2c_handle);
            gpioTerminate();
            if (init_i2c() != 0) {
                fprintf(stderr, "Failed to re-initialize I2C. Exiting.\n");
                break;
            }
            gpioInitialise();
            gpioSetMode(GPIO_LED, PI_OUTPUT);
            gpioSetPWMrange(GPIO_LED, PWM_RANGE);
            gpioSetPWMfrequency(GPIO_LED, 1000);
            continue; // Skip to the next iteration
        }

        if (current_lux > last_logged_lux + LUX_THRESHOLD) {
            if (log_lux(current_lux) == 0) {
                last_logged_lux = current_lux;
            }
        }

        // Calculate brightness based on lux value (non-linear mapping)
        brightness = (int)(current_lux / 100.0 * PWM_RANGE); // Adjust divisor as needed
        if (brightness < 0) brightness = 0;
        if (brightness > PWM_RANGE) brightness = PWM_RANGE;

        if (set_led_brightness(brightness) == 0) {
            printf("Lux: %.2f, Brightness: %d\n", current_lux, brightness);
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error setting LED brightness\n");
        }

        gpioDelay(SLEEP_DURATION * 1000); // Convert milliseconds to microseconds
    }

    // Cleanup (should not reach here due to signal handler)
    set_led_brightness(0);
    i2cClose(i2c_handle);
    gpioTerminate();

    return 0;
}

/*
To run this program, you will need to install the pigpio library:
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install pigpio

Compile with:
gcc -o light_control light_control.c -lpigpio -lrt

Run with:
sudo ./light_control
*/

/*
Compilation instructions for Raspberry Pi 5:
gcc -o light_control light_control.c -lpigpio -lrt -pthread
*/

/*
Distributed System Considerations:
The sensor data logging could be distributed across multiple nodes.
For example, one node could read sensor data and transmit it to a central server via UDP or MQTT,
where another node (or a separate application) could receive the data and control the LED strip.

Additional nodes could also be added to the system to read sensor data from different locations.
This would increase the robustness of the system by providing redundancy and increasing the coverage area.
*/</s>
